REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT TIMOTHY CANNON'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS UNTIMELY. A MOTION TO QUASH MUST BE MADE "ON OR BEFORE THE LAST DAY OF HIS OR HER TIME TO PLEAD." (C.C.P. SECTION 418.10(A)). A DEFENDANT'S LAST DAY TO PLEAD IS 30 DAYS AFTER SERVICE IS DEEMED COMPLETE, AND DEFENDANT ABACUS WAS SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS AND FAC ON FEBRUARY 10, 2025. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Aug-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TIMOTHY CANNON'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OFF CALENDAR. The motion to quash is untimely. A motion to quash must be made "on or before the last day of his or her time to plead." (C.C.P. Section 418.10(a)). A Defendant's last day to plead is 30 days after service is deemed complete, and Defendant Abacus was served with the Summons and FAC on February 10, 2025. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
